HomeDocsServerStudioComparisonsGitHub ↗

BLite.Server

Preview
🖥️Overview📦Installation🔌Protocol & API🎨Web Studio📡Client SDKs🔗Version Matrix

🔌 Protocol & API

BLite.Server exposes three interfaces: a high-throughput gRPC endpoint for the official client SDKs, a human-friendly REST API, and a BLQL query language available on both.

Authentication

Every request must carry an API key. Two header formats are accepted:

# Header-based (gRPC metadata and REST)
x-api-key: your-api-key-here

# Bearer token (REST only)
Authorization: Bearer your-api-key-here

Keys are stored as SHA-256 hex hashes in the system database. The plaintext key is returned exactly once — at creation or rotation time.

🛡 Dev Mode: When no users have been created yet (before first setup), all requests are authenticated automatically as a synthetic root user with full permissions.

Permissions

Each API key carries a list of per-collection permission entries:

FlagValueDescription
Query1Read documents and run queries
Insert2Insert new documents
Update4Update existing documents
Delete8Delete documents
Drop16Drop collections
Admin32User management and database operations
Write14Shorthand: Insert | Update | Delete
All63All flags combined

Collection "*" grants permissions on all collections. Namespace isolation is applied transparently — users never see the physical prefix.

gRPC Endpoint — Port 2626

The gRPC endpoint is the primary interface for all official client SDKs. It runs on HTTP/2 only and uses Protobuf + MessagePack internally:

  • Document payloads are transmitted as C-BSON bytes (the native BLite wire format)
  • Query descriptors are serialized with MessagePack
  • gRPC service definitions live in BLite.Proto

Available Services

ServiceDescription
DynamicServiceSchema-less operations: Insert, Update, Delete, Query, FindById, InsertBulk. Document payloads are raw BsonDocument bytes.
DocumentServiceTyped operations using the client SDK's compile-time BSON mappers. Supports streaming responses.
AdminServiceUser management (create, rotate, delete), database management. Requires Admin permission.
MetadataServiceCollection listing, index introspection, collection statistics.
TransactionServiceBegin, Commit, Rollback across any collection. One active transaction per database at a time.

Connecting with grpcurl

# List services
grpcurl -H "x-api-key: your-key" -insecure localhost:2626 list

# Call an RPC
grpcurl -H "x-api-key: your-key" -insecure \
  -d '{"collection":"orders"}' \
  localhost:2626 blite.DynamicService/Query

REST API — Port 2627

All REST endpoints live under /api/v1. A Swagger UI is available at /swagger.

Documents

MethodPathDescription
GET/{dbId}/{col}/documentsList documents (paginated)
GET/{dbId}/{col}/documents/{id}Find document by ID
POST/{dbId}/{col}/documentsInsert a document
PUT/{dbId}/{col}/documents/{id}Replace a document
DELETE/{dbId}/{col}/documents/{id}Delete a document

Collections & Databases

MethodPathDescription
GET/api/v1/databasesList all databases
POST/api/v1/databasesCreate a database
DELETE/api/v1/databases/{dbId}Drop a database
GET/api/v1/{dbId}/collectionsList collections
DELETE/api/v1/{dbId}/collections/{col}Drop a collection

Users

MethodPathDescription
GET/api/v1/usersList users (Admin)
POST/api/v1/usersCreate user (Admin)
DELETE/api/v1/users/{username}Delete user (Admin)
POST/api/v1/users/{username}/rotateRotate API key (Admin)

dbId Sentinel

Use default in the URL to target the system (default) database. For tenant databases, use the database ID assigned at creation time.

BLQL — BLite Query Language

BLQL is an MQL-inspired query language available on both the gRPC DynamicService and the REST POST /{dbId}/{col}/query endpoint.

REST Example

POST /api/v1/default/orders/query
Content-Type: application/json
x-api-key: your-key

{
  "filter": { "status": { "$eq": "active" } },
  "sort":   { "created_at": -1 },
  "skip":   0,
  "take":   20,
  "select": ["_id", "status", "total"]
}

Supported Filter Operators

OperatorDescription
$eqEqual to
$neNot equal to
$gt / $gteGreater than / greater than or equal
$lt / $lteLess than / less than or equal
$inValue is in an array
$ninValue is not in an array
$startsWithString starts with prefix
$containsString contains substring
$and / $orLogical combinators

Transactions over gRPC

Cross-collection ACID transactions are available via the TransactionService:

// Begin a transaction
var txn = await txnClient.BeginAsync(new BeginRequest { DatabaseId = "default" });

// Pass transaction ID in subsequent write requests
await dynamicClient.InsertAsync(new InsertRequest {
    Collection   = "orders",
    TransactionId = txn.TransactionId,
    Document     = bsonBytes
});

// Commit or rollback
await txnClient.CommitAsync(new CommitRequest { TransactionId = txn.TransactionId });
⚠ Important: Only one active transaction per database is allowed at a time. Always call Commit or Rollback — an abandoned transaction blocks the database until the configured Transactions:TimeoutSeconds expires.
